title: BME280 Combined Pressure, Humidity, and Temperature Sensor
content: The Bosch BME280 is a compact MEMS sensor providing barometric pressure (300–1100 hPa, ±1 hPa), relative humidity (0–100 %, ±3 % RH), and temperature (−40 to +85 °C, ±1 °C) on a single I²C (0x76/0x77) or SPI interface.
extended_content: The BME280 uses a single tiny MEMS die for all three measurements. Pressure accuracy ±1 hPa equates to ±8.3 m altitude resolution (useful for floor-level detection in buildings). Humidity response time: 1 s (τ63 %). Oversampling settings (OS1–OS16) trade conversion time for noise reduction; typical weather-station mode: OS1 for all three, filter coeff 4, standby 1000 ms. Power: 3.6 µA at 1 Hz sampling; sleep current 0.1 µA. I²C address: 0x76 (SDO=GND) or 0x77 (SDO=VCC). Arduino: Adafruit BME280 library or Bosch BME280 driver. Altitude calculation: h = 44330 × (1 − (P/P₀)^(1/5.255)); P₀ = 1013.25 hPa at sea level. Not to be confused with BME680 (adds gas resistance / air quality) or BMP280 (pressure + temperature only, no humidity).
units: h: m, P: hPa, P₀: hPa
source: Bosch Sensortec BME280 Data Sheet v1.24; Adafruit BME280 Library Documentation
